questions.q: Kanishka was the most renowned ruler of which empire?
questions.explanation: Kanishka was the greatest ruler of the Kushan Empire.

questions.q: Around which century CE did Kanishka reign?
questions.explanation: Kanishka is generally placed in the early 2nd century CE, with his accession often dated to around 127 CE.

questions.q: Which religion did Kanishka famously patronise?
questions.explanation: Kanishka is celebrated as a great patron of Buddhism.

questions.q: Kanishka's realm spanned Central Asia and which other major region?
questions.explanation: The Kushan Empire under Kanishka extended over Central Asia and northern India.

questions.q: Which Buddhist council is traditionally associated with Kanishka?
questions.explanation: Kanishka is traditionally linked with the Fourth Buddhist Council, said to have been held in Kashmir.

questions.q: The flourishing of which famous art style is associated with Kanishka's reign?
questions.explanation: Kanishka's era saw the flourishing of Gandharan art, blending Greek and Indian styles.

questions.q: Kanishka belonged to which group of people who had migrated into the region from Central Asia?
questions.explanation: The Kushans descended from the Yuezhi, a Central Asian people who moved into Bactria and beyond.

questions.q: In which century is Kanishka most often placed by historians?
questions.explanation: Kanishka is most commonly dated to the early 2nd century CE.

questions.q: Kanishka is best remembered as a patron of religion and what else?
questions.explanation: Kanishka was a patron of both Buddhism and the arts, especially the Gandhara school.

questions.q: Kanishka's empire is best described as straddling which two broad areas?
options: Central Asia and the Indian subcontinent
questions.explanation: The Kushan realm under Kanishka linked Central Asia with the northern Indian subcontinent.

questions.q: Which city, in present-day Pakistan, is generally regarded as Kanishka's principal capital?
questions.explanation: Kanishka's main capital was Purushapura, modern Peshawar.

questions.q: Kanishka is credited with founding a calendar era that began in which year, by the most widely accepted dating?
questions.explanation: A widely accepted scholarly view places the start of the Kanishka era around 127 CE.

questions.q: The famous Bimaran and other reliquaries aside, Kanishka is associated with a great relic tower (stupa) at which city?
questions.explanation: Kanishka built a celebrated multi-storeyed stupa at Purushapura (Peshawar), praised by later Chinese pilgrims.

questions.q: Kanishka's coins depict deities from several traditions; this reflects what about his empire?
questions.explanation: Kushan coins show Greek, Iranian, and Indian deities, reflecting the empire's cosmopolitan and tolerant culture.

questions.q: Which renowned Buddhist scholar and poet is traditionally associated with Kanishka's court?
questions.explanation: The poet and philosopher Ashvaghosha, author of the Buddhacharita, is traditionally linked to Kanishka's court.

questions.q: Kanishka's patronage helped spread which form of Buddhism along the trade routes to Central Asia and China?
questions.explanation: The Kushan period, especially under Kanishka, is associated with the rise and spread of Mahayana Buddhism.

questions.q: The Kushan Empire under Kanishka profited greatly from controlling part of which trade network?
questions.explanation: Kushan control of key routes made the empire a major beneficiary of Silk Road trade between East and West.

questions.q: On many of his gold coins, in which script and language do Kanishka's later legends appear?
options: Greek script writing the Bactrian language
questions.explanation: Kanishka's coinage shifted to using the Greek script to write the Iranian Bactrian language.

questions.q: Two principal schools of Buddhist sculpture flourished under the Kushans: Gandhara and which other?
questions.explanation: Alongside Gandhara, the Mathura school of sculpture flourished under Kushan patronage.

questions.q: A famous headless statue of Kanishka, showing him in Central Asian dress, was found at which site?
questions.explanation: A well-known standing statue of Kanishka, now headless, was discovered at the Kushan dynastic shrine at Mat near Mathura.

questions.q: Which physician, traditionally said to have served at Kanishka's court, is associated with Ayurvedic medicine?
questions.explanation: Tradition associates the great medical author Charaka with Kanishka's court, though the link is uncertain.

questions.q: The Rabatak inscription, key to Kanishka's genealogy, is written in which language?
questions.explanation: The Rabatak inscription, found in Afghanistan, is in the Bactrian language and lists Kanishka's royal ancestors.

questions.q: According to the Rabatak inscription, who was Kanishka's father?
questions.explanation: The Rabatak inscription names Kanishka's father as Vima Kadphises, and his grandfather as Vima Takto.

questions.q: Who is generally regarded as Kanishka's successor on the Kushan throne?
questions.explanation: Kanishka I was succeeded by Huvishka, under whom the Kushan Empire continued to flourish.

questions.q: The Fourth Buddhist Council under Kanishka is traditionally said to have been presided over by which scholar?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Vasumitra presided over the Fourth Council, with Ashvaghosha assisting, under Kanishka's patronage.

questions.q: The Kanishka casket, a famous Kushan reliquary, was discovered at the site of which ancient city?
questions.explanation: The gilded Kanishka casket was unearthed at Shah-ji-ki-Dheri near Peshawar, the site of his great stupa.

questions.q: The Bactrian language used on Kanishka's coins and inscriptions belongs to which language family branch?
questions.explanation: Bactrian is an Eastern Iranian language, though it was unusually written in the Greek alphabet.

questions.q: A small number of Kanishka's coins bear the earliest dated image of the Buddha with a legend reading what?
questions.explanation: Rare Kanishka coins show a standing Buddha labelled 'Boddo' in Greek script, among the earliest such depictions.

questions.q: Which Chinese pilgrim-era accounts and later sources describe Kanishka's towering relic stupa at Peshawar?
questions.explanation: Chinese pilgrims Faxian and Xuanzang described the great Kanishka stupa at Purushapura as a wonder of the Buddhist world.

questions.q: Besides the Buddha, Kanishka's coins prominently feature which Iranian solar or fire-related deities?
questions.explanation: Kanishka's coinage includes Iranian deities such as Miiro (Mithra, the sun) and Atsho (the fire god), alongside others.
